info.name: tms-config
info.description:
## 20230901

Added Rules 024 and 026

Integrated rules into typologies

Removed non-viable rules and typologies from network map

Updated transaction configuration structure to remove array

## 20230905

Added typology 137 (transaction limit evaluation) back

## 20230915

Removed duplicated typologies 45 and 107 from the network map

Added rule 045 to typology 028

## 20231005

Added a "cfg" attribute to the network map

Created separate configurations for rule 078 for pain.001/013 enablement

## 20231009

Added a workflow object to the typology configuration

Migrated interdiction thresholds to the workflow.alertThreshold attribute (66% of the number of rules)

Added a workflow.interdictionThreshold attribute to all fraud typologies (80% of the number of rules)

Deprecated the Transaction Configuration

## 20231019

Updated the typology configurations and network map to decouple of the typology configuration from the typology processor

## 20231020

Bug: network map typology configuration had two id fields instead of a cfg field

Bug: The final band descriptions in rules 044 and 045 did not match the threshold

Added rule 078 for pain001/013 disabled as the _override_

## 20231023

Bug: fixed the composition of the typology id in the network map

## 20240422

Updated network map to remove the channel branching

Update the rule configs to remove the "outcome" attribute

Update the typology configs to replace the true/false weighting attributes with a weighting attribute

## 20240621

Bug: Removed the activation of the network map step since it was causing two active network maps.

Updated the fetch of the network map ID to also deactive ALL active network maps

Removed the step to deactivate a specific network map by ID

## 20240716 - Tazama 2.0.0

Implemented MathJSON formulas

Implemented 1-to-many typology config rule results array

Reinstated the full network map

## 20240927 - Tazama 3.0.0

Added the Event-Flow Rule Processor to the network map

Added the EFRuP results to interdicting typologies to allow for overrides  
Added a dedicated EFRuP typology to ensure the EFRuP is always running, even if not in any typologies

## 20241007 - Tazama 3.0.0 fixes

Fixed missing interdiction thresholds from typology configuration

Added "none" outcome to EFRuP rule results

## 20250929

Refactored configuration for multitenancy

## 20251031

Implemented PostgreSQL

item.name: Truncate all PostgreSQL configuration tables for a specific tenant

"desc": "Use of several currencies, structured transactions, with a great number of persons involved, large number of transactions related to each other during a short time period.",
"desc": "Receiving funds from high number of senders over a short period of time, large sum transactions compared to person's living standard.",

"desc": "Large volume of transactions for the same customer with multiple instances of using different name spellings, false addresses or identification that evolves i.e. some parts of the identification change, while other parts remains the same, such as a person whose last name changes while his first name, date of birth, identification number and address remain the same.",

"desc": "Unusual ratio of sent to received transactions (the direction of the flow of the suspicious ratio imbalance being determined by the context). Large inbound transaction with multiple small withdrawals or payments to multiple individual beneficiaries. Multiple small deposits are made into the account with a single large deposit to another account or large withdrawal from the ATM.",
"desc": "Series of transactions structured just below the threshold in order to prevent the transaction being reported to the regulatory authority.",
"desc": "Money transfers from one account to numerous unrelated accounts. The immediate remittance or transfer of funds once they are deposited into an account.",

"desc": "Holding a large number of accounts with the same Internet payment services provider allows for a customer to access multiple channels through which a transaction is able to be performed. With multiple transacting channels a fraudster or money laundering can structure their activity in order to make it appear more legitimate.",
"desc": "Large number of transfers between a group of individuals and accounts made in a systematic way",
"desc": "False promotions, phishing, or social engineering scams.",
"desc": "Mobile Network Operator Employees/Agents transferring customer funds to personal account.",
"desc": "Fraudster takes over the account of a legitimate customer and uses monies/credit facilities in them.",

"desc": "Fraudsters use the personal details of a victim to create bank accounts and apply for credit. The account is then utilised to withdraw the funds or immediate transactions are performed soon after the account is opened",
"desc": "Fraudsters secure mobile wallet or account details of victims and conduct user-not-present fraud.",
"desc": "Performing similar transactions (i.e. cash deposits) at multiple branches of the same institution on the same business day",
"desc": "Accounts of salaried employees credited outside of normal salary scope",
"desc": "Accounts that show unexpectedly large cash deposits and immediate withdrawals",
"desc": "Duplication of payments from a single account.",
"desc": "Customer receives many small incoming wire transfers and then orders a large outgoing wire transfer.",
"desc": "Associations with multiple accounts under multiple names. Use of aliases for the purpose of opening multiple accounts in different banks, or in different branches of the same bank",
"desc": "Elaborate movement of funds through different accounts. This may be highlighted through transactional activity on different accounts belonging to the same customer.",
"desc": "Unexplained deposits into the bank account of an unemployed spouse or minor.",

"desc": "Large/frequent cash deposits into accounts. This risk can also occur where there are rapid deposits of the same amount into the account. Generally where the deposit is difficult to explain. There are also transfers into the account from other entities. Large amount of cash from unexplained sources. This can also include a large volume of cash and / or cheque deposits into the bank account on a regular basis. Often this is followed up with a number of cheques drawn on the account or suspicious withdrawals.",
"desc": "Transaction in excess of the country's reporting threshold.",
"desc": "Transaction pattern is not in line with the past transactional patterns. This can be in the value and volume of transactions.",
"desc": "The structuring of transactions using the same beneficiaries and a large number of transactions during a short time period.",
